steps.description: Face the front end of the 65mm diameter raw stock to establish the Z=0 reference point and ensure a flat surface.
Perform an outer diameter roughing pass to reduce the entire length of the stock from Ø65 mm to the maximum part diameter of Ø60 mm.
Step turn the section from Z=45 mm to Z=145 mm down to a diameter of Ø40 mm.
Step turn the section from Z=90 mm to Z=145 mm down to a diameter of Ø20 mm.
Machine the first taper transition from Ø60 mm at Z=45 mm to Ø40 mm at Z=70 mm.
Machine the second taper transition from Ø40 mm at Z=90 mm to Ø20 mm at Z=115 mm.
Machine the final taper at the tip from Ø20 mm at Z=130 mm to Ø5 mm at Z=145 mm.
Perform a continuous finish pass across the entire profile (Ø60, Taper, Ø40, Taper, Ø20, Taper) to achieve final dimensions and surface finish.
Cut the finished part from the remaining stock at the total length of 145 mm.

reasoning: The part is a complex shaft consisting of three cylindrical steps (Ø60, Ø40, Ø20) connected by two tapered sections, ending in a final conical taper. The raw stock is Ø65 x 150mm cast iron. The strategy involves first preparing the stock by facing and reducing the overall diameter to the maximum required size (Ø60). Then, a sequence of step turning operations is used to remove bulk material for the smaller diameters. Taper turning is applied specifically to the transition zones defined in the drawing. Finally, a finishing pass ensures the profile is smooth before parting the part to its final 145mm length.
step_by_step_plan: 1. Secure the Ø65mm x 150mm cast iron bar in the lathe chuck with approximately 148mm protruding. 2. Face the end to set Z-zero. 3. Turn the entire outer diameter to Ø60mm. 4. Perform step turning to reduce the diameter to Ø40mm starting from 45mm from the face. 5. Perform a second step turn to reduce the diameter to Ø20mm starting from 90mm from the face. 6. Program the taper from Z=45 to Z=70 (Ø60 to Ø40). 7. Program the taper from Z=90 to Z=115 (Ø40 to Ø20). 8. Program the final taper from Z=130 to Z=145 (Ø20 to Ø5). 9. Run a finishing cycle over the entire geometry. 10. Use the parting tool at Z=145 to separate the finished part from the excess stock.
